title: phlox carolina subsp. triflora
intro: Phlox carolina subsp. triflora, also known as the Carolina phlox, is a plant species within the phlox genus, native to the southeastern United States. Traditionally used in herbal medicine, Phlox carolina subsp. triflora has been found to exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal value. The plant's health benefits are thought to include reducing fever, calming digestive issues, and alleviating respiratory problems. Herbal preparations involving Phlox carolina subsp. triflora typically involve making a tea from its flowers or leaves, or using it in tinctures and infusions.
